skillName: Use formal methods for addition of integers
misconceptionSummary: Students add digits without carrying, writing two-digit sums in a single column.

lines.text: Align ones under ones, tens under tens…
Start adding from the ones column

steps.narration: Write the numbers with matching columns aligned — ones under ones, tens under tens, hundreds under hundreds. Always start adding from the ones column on the right.
When a column sums to ten or more, write the units digit and carry the tens digit to the next column. The carried digit is added in with that column's total.
Add the thousands including any final carry. Three plus one plus the carried one gives five. The answer is five thousand one hundred and twelve.

visuals.label: Align → start right → add column by column → carry tens

steps.narration: Five thousand one hundred and twelve. Column addition with careful carrying gives the exact answer every time.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: writing 12 in the ones column instead of carrying the 1. Each column holds only one digit — when the sum reaches 10 or more, carry the tens digit left.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Each column can hold only one digit. When a column sums to 10 or more, write the units digit and carry the ten into the next column to the left.
